entries.definition:
f–r–ʿ–n Pharaoh; to act tyrannically, to oppress; sagacity,
intelligence, cunning; crocodile. Some philologists derive فرعون
firʿawn from this root, but the majority recognise it as a
borrowing from the language of the Copts, with other meanings,
presumably, derived from it. Of this root, فرعون firʿawn, occurs
74 times in the Qur’an.

فِرْعَوْن firʿawn [n.] Pharaoh, king of Egypt (10:79) وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ
ائْتُونِي بِكُلِّ سَاحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ and Pharaoh said, ‘Bring me every learned
sorcerer.’
